title: Adjusting Entries — The Four Families
event: Hollis Co. reaches December 31 with one adjustment pending. Prepare the adjusting entry.

options.label: Prepaid: $24,000 rent paid Sep 1 for 12 months
Unearned: $15,000 received Aug 1 for 10 months of service
Accrued expense: 9% interest on a $100,000 note, 4 months old
Accrued revenue: $6,200 of services performed, unbilled

lines.label: 24,000 × 4/12
lines.trap: Count months from SEPTEMBER 1 — four have passed, not three.

lines.label: asset used
lines.why: The asset drains as the space is used; $16,000 remains for next year.
lines.trap: Credit the prepaid, never Cash — cash left in September.

lines.label: 15,000 × 5/10
lines.why: Five months (Aug–Dec) of the obligation are worked off.
lines.trap: Debiting a liability to shrink it — the direction that trips people.

lines.label: earned to date
lines.why: Half the contract is now earned income.
lines.trap: The other 7,500 stays parked as a liability into next year.

lines.label: principal × rate × time
lines.why: Four months of borrowing cost belong to this year.
lines.trap: The fraction is the whole battle: 4/12 of a FULL year's 9,000.

lines.label: owed, unpaid
lines.why: The obligation accrues even though no payment is due yet.
lines.trap: Payable, not Cash — accruals record cost BEFORE cash moves.

lines.label: earned, unbilled
lines.why: The claim exists the moment the work is done.
lines.trap: Unbilled ≠ unearned — the invoice is paperwork, not the earning event.

lines.label: work performed
lines.why: December's work belongs in December's income.
lines.trap: Omitting this understates revenue AND assets — a favorite 'find the missing adjustment.'

memorize.body: Four families: two deferrals (cash first — prepaids, unearned) and two accruals (cash later — accrued expenses, accrued revenues).
Every adjusting entry pairs one income-statement account with one balance-sheet account, and NEVER touches Cash.
The time fraction is where the points are lost — anchor to the exact start date and count on your fingers.

questions.prompt: $100,000 note at 9%, outstanding 4 months at year-end. Accrued interest = ?

distractors.feedback: That's a FULL year. Only 4 months have run: 9,000 × 4/12 = 3,000.
That's one month. Four have accrued since the note was signed.

build.scaffold: Name the family first (deferral vs accrual, expense vs revenue) — the accounts then pick themselves.
